candidates.name: Michelle Lujan Grisham
candidates.notes: 32nd Governor of New Mexico (2019-). Catholic. First Democratic Latina governor in U.S. history. Signed sweeping gun control measures including temporary suspension of concealed carry in Albuquerque (controversial). Signed legislation codifying abortion rights. Pro-immigration. Former U.S. House member. [2026 actions: TERM-LIMITED — cannot seek re-election to third consecutive term per NM Constitution. NM gubernatorial race wide open Nov 2026.]

profile.education: University of New Mexico (BA, University Studies); University of New Mexico School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Attorney and health administrator; U.S. Representative (2013-2019); became 32nd governor in 2019.
profile.confidence_note: Federal expansion rescore — establishment_d archetype with individual overrides where named records diverge. claims.text: In September 2023 invoked emergency public-health authority to suspend open and concealed carry throughout Albuquerque and Bernalillo County for 30 days — an unprecedented attempt to override Second Amendment rights by executive fiat. A federal judge immediately blocked the order as unconstitutional. She subsequently signed HB 129 in 2024, imposing a statewide 7-day waiting period on all firearm purchases, continuing her anti-gun legislative agenda.

Endorsed by NARAL Pro-Choice America (now Reproductive Freedom for All) and carries their highest rating; signed SB 10 in February 2021 repealing New Mexico's 1969 abortion law, making abortion legal through all stages of pregnancy; issued executive orders after Dobbs (2022) to shield abortion providers from prosecution; applauded the 2025 New Mexico Supreme Court ruling striking down local municipal abortion restrictions — placing her squarely within the abortion-industry endorsement network the rubric identifies as disqualifying.

profile.background: Mechanical engineer and former Albuquerque City Councilman. Former U.S. House member. Worked as field engineer and natural resources trustee for New Mexico.

candidates.notes: Junior Senator from New Mexico (2021-). Roman Catholic. Heritage Action lifetime ~4%. NRA F. Former U.S. House member (2009-2021) and Assistant Speaker. profile.background: Former U.S. House member and Assistant Speaker. Former New Mexico Public Regulation Commissioner. Son of longtime state House Speaker Ben Lujan. Worked as engineer and IT professional.
